tools.name: agent_advance_phase
tools.firstSentence: Spec 034: advance an artifact to a target phase (1..7) in the seven-phase RE workflow.
tools.desc: Spec 034: advance an artifact to a target phase (1..7) in the seven-phase RE workflow. Skipping more than one phase forward requires an evidence string. Cannot move backward.

tools.name: agent_freeze_artifact
tools.firstSentence: Spec 034: freeze an artifact at its current phase (cracker mode for asset PRGs / level data that has no relevance to the crack).
tools.desc: Spec 034: freeze an artifact at its current phase (cracker mode for asset PRGs / level data that has no relevance to the crack). Frozen artifacts skip propose_next and count as 'done' for completion math.

tools.name: agent_onboard
tools.firstSentence: Reload the full project state into the session.
tools.desc: Reload the full project state into the session. Use when a session starts or after context loss/compaction — run it FIRST. Not for per-turn checks (use c64re_whats_next) or choosing among options (use agent_propose_next). Inputs: none. Returns: project metadata, workflow phases, agent role/state, recent artifacts, open tasks + questions, and the proposed next action.

tools.name: agent_propose_next
tools.firstSentence: List ranked, phase-consistent next actions across artifacts, read-only.
tools.desc: List ranked, phase-consistent next actions across artifacts, read-only. Use when you want to choose among options or see what each artifact needs. Not for the single routine next step (use c64re_whats_next) or full reload (use agent_onboard). Inputs: none. Returns: ranked actions per non-frozen artifact + optional worker-subagent suggestion. Does not mutate state.

tools.name: agent_record_step
tools.firstSentence: Record a completed work step and optionally queue the next action, so a later session can resume.
tools.desc: Record a completed work step and optionally queue the next action, so a later session can resume. Use after each meaningful unit of work. Not for proposing options (use agent_propose_next) or per-turn status (use c64re_whats_next). Inputs: step summary, optional next-action. Returns: updated agent state.

tools.name: agent_set_role
tools.firstSentence: Set the working role for this session — analyst, cartographer, or implementer — which biases what agent_propose_next recommends.
tools.desc: Set the working role for this session — analyst, cartographer, or implementer — which biases what agent_propose_next recommends. Use when you begin focused work in one of those modes. Not for recording progress (use agent_record_step). Inputs: role. Returns: confirmation + persisted role.

tools.name: analyze_g64_anomalies
tools.firstSentence: Scan a G64 image track-by-track and report duplicate, missing, unexpected, or invalid decoded sectors.
tools.desc: Scan a G64 image track-by-track and report duplicate, missing, unexpected, or invalid decoded sectors.

tools.name: analyze_prg
tools.firstSentence: Run the heuristic analysis pipeline on a PRG and produce structured JSON — segments, cross-references, RAM facts, pointer tables.
tools.desc: Run the heuristic analysis pipeline on a PRG and produce structured JSON — segments, cross-references, RAM facts, pointer tables. Use first on any new PRG to map its structure. Not for producing assembly (run disasm_prg next, passing this JSON) or for disk/cart images (extract first). Inputs: prg_path, optional project_dir. Returns: analysis JSON path + summary.

tools.name: apply_patch_recipe
tools.firstSentence: Spec 027: apply a patch recipe.
tools.desc: Spec 027: apply a patch recipe. Refuses if expected bytes do not match the target unless allow_mismatch is set. Snapshots prior bytes via Spec 025 versioning.

tools.name: archive_phase1_noise
tools.firstSentence: Spec 053 (Bug 20): walk hypothesis-kind findings with addressRange, archive any that fall fully inside a routine annotation finding's addressRange.
tools.desc: Spec 053 (Bug 20): walk hypothesis-kind findings with addressRange, archive any that fall fully inside a routine annotation finding's addressRange. Also closes paired heuristic-phase1 questions whose title address matches. dry_run=true previews without writing. Spec 056 R27: pass `artifact_id` to scope routines + hypothesis candidates + questions to those linked to a single artifact (per-file feedback signal).

tools.name: assemble_source
tools.firstSentence: Assemble a .asm (KickAssembler) or .tass (64tass) file to a binary, optionally byte-comparing the rebuild against the original PRG.
tools.desc: Assemble a .asm (KickAssembler) or .tass (64tass) file to a binary, optionally byte-comparing the rebuild against the original PRG. Use to verify a disassembly rebuilds correctly. Not for generating the source (use disasm_prg). Inputs: source path, optional original PRG. Returns: assembled binary path + cmp result.

tools.name: auto_resolve_questions
tools.firstSentence: Spec 052: run the catch-up sweep across all auto-resolvable questions (Pfad A + B).
tools.desc: Spec 052: run the catch-up sweep across all auto-resolvable questions (Pfad A + B). Pfad C runs from the annotation-save endpoint. Returns counts. Spec 056 R27: pass `artifact_id` to scope the sweep to questions/findings linked to that artifact only — useful as a per-file feedback signal after annotation work.

tools.name: auto_tag_relevance
tools.firstSentence: Spec 041: heuristic-classify all artifacts and propose relevance tags (loader / protection / save / kernal / asset).
tools.desc: Spec 041: heuristic-classify all artifacts and propose relevance tags (loader / protection / save / kernal / asset). Suggestions only — call set_artifact_relevance to apply. dry_run=true (default) returns proposals without writing.

tools.name: backfill_finding_address_ranges
tools.firstSentence: Bug 28: walk findings.json and copy evidence[0].addressRange to top-level addressRange when missing.
tools.desc: Bug 28: walk findings.json and copy evidence[0].addressRange to top-level addressRange when missing. One-shot migration for projects whose hypothesis findings only have evidence-level ranges. Returns count updated. Idempotent.

tools.name: backfill_internal_flags
tools.firstSentence: Bug 26 / Spec 058 follow-up: walk artifacts + entities and set `internal: true` on records where the heuristic (path / role / kind) classifies them as infrastru
tools.desc: Bug 26 / Spec 058 follow-up: walk artifacts + entities and set `internal: true` on records where the heuristic (path / role / kind) classifies them as infrastructure but the flag was never persisted (legacy data predating the schema field). Idempotent. Entity classification uses the primary linked artifact's flag after the artifact pass. dry_run=true previews without writing.

tools.name: backfill_manifest_payload_hashes
tools.firstSentence: Bug 33 Fix A (manifest path): walk every artifact of kind=manifest, re-parse it, resolve each entry's file path to bytes, compute sha256, and write back into th
tools.desc: Bug 33 Fix A (manifest path): walk every artifact of kind=manifest, re-parse it, resolve each entry's file path to bytes, compute sha256, and write back into the matching manifest-imported entity's payloadContentHash. Matches entities by the stableId pattern that manifest-import uses. Skips already-hashed entities. dry_run=true previews. Idempotent.

tools.name: backfill_payload_content_hashes
tools.firstSentence: Bug 33 Fix A: walk payload-bearing entities (kind=payload OR payloadLoadAddress set), and for each whose payloadContentHash is null AND payloadSourceArtifactId
tools.desc: Bug 33 Fix A: walk payload-bearing entities (kind=payload OR payloadLoadAddress set), and for each whose payloadContentHash is null AND payloadSourceArtifactId points at a directly-linked file (NOT a manifest/aggregator), read the file bytes and compute sha256, writing back into entity.payloadContentHash. For manifest-sourced entities use backfill_manifest_payload_hashes instead. Skips already-hashed entities. dry_run=true previews without writing. Idempotent.

tools.name: backfill_question_address_ranges
tools.firstSentence: Bug 29: walk open-questions.json and copy the linked finding's addressRange (or evidence[0].addressRange as fallback) to the question's top-level addressRange w
tools.desc: Bug 29: walk open-questions.json and copy the linked finding's addressRange (or evidence[0].addressRange as fallback) to the question's top-level addressRange when missing. One-shot migration. Returns count updated. Idempotent.

tools.name: build_all_views
tools.firstSentence: Rebuild + persist ALL project JSON view-models in one pass (dashboard, memory map, listing, …) for the UI.
tools.desc: Rebuild + persist ALL project JSON view-models in one pass (dashboard, memory map, listing, …) for the UI. Use after knowledge changes to refresh every view at once. Not for human-readable markdown (use render_docs) or a single view (use the specific build_* tool). Inputs: none. Returns: rebuilt view list.

tools.name: build_annotated_listing_view
tools.firstSentence: Rebuild + persist the JSON annotated-listing view-model (disasm + annotations) for the UI.
tools.desc: Rebuild + persist the JSON annotated-listing view-model (disasm + annotations) for the UI. Use to refresh just the listing view. Not for all views (use build_all_views) or markdown (use render_docs). Inputs: none. Returns: listing view path.

tools.name: build_cartridge_layout_view
tools.firstSentence: Build and persist the JSON cartridge-layout view-model for the current project.
tools.desc: Build and persist the JSON cartridge-layout view-model for the current project.

tools.name: build_disk_layout_view
tools.firstSentence: Build and persist the JSON disk-layout view-model for the current project.
tools.desc: Build and persist the JSON disk-layout view-model for the current project.

tools.name: build_flow_graph_view
tools.firstSentence: Build and persist the JSON flow-graph view-model for the current project.
tools.desc: Build and persist the JSON flow-graph view-model for the current project.

tools.name: build_load_sequence_view
tools.firstSentence: Build and persist the JSON load-sequence view-model for the current project.
tools.desc: Build and persist the JSON load-sequence view-model for the current project.

tools.name: build_memory_map
tools.firstSentence: Rebuild + persist the JSON memory-map view-model (address-space layout) for the UI.
tools.desc: Rebuild + persist the JSON memory-map view-model (address-space layout) for the UI. Use to refresh just the memory map. Not for all views (use build_all_views). Inputs: none. Returns: memory-map view path.

tools.name: build_project_dashboard
tools.firstSentence: Rebuild + persist the JSON dashboard view-model (project status overview) for the UI.
tools.desc: Rebuild + persist the JSON dashboard view-model (project status overview) for the UI. Use to refresh just the dashboard. Not for all views (use build_all_views) or markdown (use render_docs). Inputs: none. Returns: dashboard view path.

tools.name: build_tools
tools.firstSentence: Compile the TRXDis pipeline (npm run build).
tools.desc: Compile the TRXDis pipeline (npm run build). Must be called before analysis if source has changed.

tools.name: bulk_create_cart_chunk_payloads
tools.firstSentence: Walk the cartridge-layout view's lutChunks and create a payload entity for each one.
tools.desc: Walk the cartridge-layout view's lutChunks and create a payload entity for each one. Idempotent (skip when an existing payload entity has matching cart-chunk:<key> tag). Closes the gap where stock cart chunks are not auto-promoted to payload entities by extract_crt.

tools.name: bulk_import_analysis_reports
tools.firstSentence: Walk every analysis-run artifact in the project and call import_analysis_report on those whose entities are not yet back-linked.
tools.desc: Walk every analysis-run artifact in the project and call import_analysis_report on those whose entities are not yet back-linked. Closes the gap that opens when bulk CLI runs (`dist/pipeline/cli.cjs analyze-prg`) register the analysis JSON but never invoke the entity / finding importer. After this runs, the loadSequence Payload-Focus dropdown in the workspace UI populates with non-empty stages and memory-map filtering becomes meaningful again.

tools.name: c64re_whats_next
tools.firstSentence: Return the single next required action for the active artifact, cheaply, to call every turn.
tools.desc: Return the single next required action for the active artifact, cheaply, to call every turn. Use as the routine per-turn nudge. Not for full state reload (use agent_onboard) or ranked option lists (use agent_propose_next). Inputs: optional artifact id. Returns: current phase, the one next action, optional worker-spawn block, audit warnings, a short reminder.

tools.name: c64ref_build_rom_knowledge
tools.firstSentence: Fetch and rebuild the local BASIC/KERNAL ROM knowledge snapshot from mist64/c64ref sources.
tools.desc: Fetch and rebuild the local BASIC/KERNAL ROM knowledge snapshot from mist64/c64ref sources.

tools.name: c64ref_lookup
tools.firstSentence: Look up C64 BASIC/KERNAL ROM knowledge by address or search term from the local reference snapshot.
tools.desc: Look up C64 BASIC/KERNAL ROM knowledge by address or search term from the local reference snapshot. Use to identify a ROM routine/vector while reading disassembly. Not for project-specific labels (use list_findings / list_entities). Inputs: address or query string. Returns: matching ROM entries.

tools.name: close_completed_tasks
tools.firstSentence: Spec 038: walk auto-suggested NEXT-hint tasks and close those whose autoCloseHint (file-exists / artifact-registered / phase-reached) is satisfied.
tools.desc: Spec 038: walk auto-suggested NEXT-hint tasks and close those whose autoCloseHint (file-exists / artifact-registered / phase-reached) is satisfied. Idempotent. Also runs automatically in agent_onboard.

tools.name: compare_exomizer_shared_encoding_sets
tools.firstSentence: Compare one or more shared-encoding manifest sets, e.g.
tools.desc: Compare one or more shared-encoding manifest sets, e.g. global vs 2-cluster vs 4-cluster, by total bytes, payload bytes, and encoding overhead.

tools.name: confirm_question_resolution
tools.firstSentence: Spec 052: confirm or reject a resolution-pending question.
tools.desc: Spec 052: confirm or reject a resolution-pending question. accept=true marks it answered with the proposed summary; accept=false flips it back to open with a rejection note.

tools.name: declare_loader_entrypoint
tools.firstSentence: Spec 028: declare a loader entry point on an artifact (jump-table, sector-load, container-decode, dispatch, init, other).
tools.desc: Spec 028: declare a loader entry point on an artifact (jump-table, sector-load, container-decode, dispatch, init, other). Idempotent on (artifact_id, address, kind).

tools.name: dedupe_artifact_registry
tools.firstSentence: Spec 060 / Bug 30: collapse legacy duplicate artifact registrations (same absolute path, different ids) into one survivor per path.
tools.desc: Spec 060 / Bug 30: collapse legacy duplicate artifact registrations (same absolute path, different ids) into one survivor per path. Survivor = oldest createdAt; merges union sourceArtifactIds / entityIds / tags / evidence / loadContexts / versions; references across entities / findings / relations / flows / tasks / open-questions remapped from deprecated ids to survivor ids. dry_run=true previews counts + first 10 sample groups without writing. Idempotent.

tools.name: dedupe_payload_entities
tools.firstSentence: Spec 060 / Bug 31: collapse legacy duplicate payload-bearing entities into one survivor per (payloadContentHash) or (payloadSourceArtifactId + payloadLoadAddres
tools.desc: Spec 060 / Bug 31: collapse legacy duplicate payload-bearing entities into one survivor per (payloadContentHash) or (payloadSourceArtifactId + payloadLoadAddress). Survivor preference: kind==payload first, then earliest createdAt. Other names fold into survivor.aliases[]. Manifest-source entities (linked artifact internal=true) are marked internal rather than removed. References across entities, findings, relations, flows, tasks, open-questions, artifacts remap from deprecated entity ids to survivor ids. dry_run=true previews counts + first 10 sample groups without writing. Idempotent.

tools.name: define_runtime_scenario
tools.firstSentence: Spec 030 / R20: define a named scenario (target artifact, breakpoints, stop condition, expected milestone).
tools.desc: Spec 030 / R20: define a named scenario (target artifact, breakpoints, stop condition, expected milestone). Used by run_runtime_scenario + diff_scenario_runs to compare original vs port builds.

tools.name: depack_bwc_bitstream
tools.firstSentence: Depack a BWC bit-stream chunk (Pucrunch-derived format with 'pu' magic).
tools.desc: Depack a BWC bit-stream chunk (Pucrunch-derived format with 'pu' magic). Runs the original $C992 depacker in the sandbox and captures the contiguous run of writes at the destination. Returns a PRG with the destination as load address.

tools.name: depack_bwc_chunk
tools.firstSentence: Auto-dispatch on the first two bytes: 'pu' magic → depack_bwc_bitstream, else → depack_bwc_raw.
tools.desc: Auto-dispatch on the first two bytes: 'pu' magic → depack_bwc_bitstream, else → depack_bwc_raw. Mirrors the engine's discriminator at $0D79.

tools.name: depack_bwc_raw
tools.firstSentence: Depack a BWC raw chunk (uncompressed).
tools.desc: Depack a BWC raw chunk (uncompressed). Layout: dest_lo / dest_hi / skip / length_pages / body[length_pages * 256]. Returns a PRG with the destination as load address.

tools.name: depack_byteboozer
tools.firstSentence: Decompress a ByteBoozer2 raw .b2 file or executable wrapper in pure TypeScript.
tools.desc: Decompress a ByteBoozer2 raw .b2 file or executable wrapper in pure TypeScript.

tools.name: depack_byteboozer_lykia
tools.firstSentence: Decompress a Lykia-variant ByteBoozer2 stream (modified 4-byte header: dest_lo, dest_hi, end_lo, end_hi; BB2_BITBUF seeded from supplied dest_hi).
tools.desc: Decompress a Lykia-variant ByteBoozer2 stream (modified 4-byte header: dest_lo, dest_hi, end_lo, end_hi; BB2_BITBUF seeded from supplied dest_hi). Pure TypeScript port of the $020C in-game depacker.

tools.name: depack_exomizer_raw
tools.firstSentence: Decompress an Exomizer raw stream via the built-in TypeScript implementation.
tools.desc: Decompress an Exomizer raw stream via the built-in TypeScript implementation.

tools.name: depack_exomizer_sfx
tools.firstSentence: Decompress an Exomizer self-extracting wrapper via the built-in TypeScript 6502-emulated depacker.
tools.desc: Decompress an Exomizer self-extracting wrapper via the built-in TypeScript 6502-emulated depacker.

tools.name: depack_rle
tools.firstSentence: Decompress the built-in C64 RLE format used by Mike's loader.
tools.desc: Decompress the built-in C64 RLE format used by Mike's loader.

tools.name: diff_scenario_runs
tools.firstSentence: Spec 030: diff two recorded scenario runs (baseline vs candidate).
tools.desc: Spec 030: diff two recorded scenario runs (baseline vs candidate). Emits + persists a RuntimeDiff with missingLoads, extraLoads, diffDestination, diffPayloadHash, divergentPc.

tools.name: disasm_menu
tools.firstSentence: Disassemble every payload in an extracted menu/multi-file container to KickAssembler sources at once.
tools.desc: Disassemble every payload in an extracted menu/multi-file container to KickAssembler sources at once. Use after extracting a menu disk/cart to get assembly for all entries. Not for a single PRG (use disasm_prg). Inputs: manifest / project dir. Returns: generated .asm paths.

tools.name: disasm_prg
tools.firstSentence: Disassemble a PRG to KickAssembler .asm + 64tass .tass, segment-aware when given an analysis JSON.
tools.desc: Disassemble a PRG to KickAssembler .asm + 64tass .tass, segment-aware when given an analysis JSON. Use after analyze_prg to get readable assembly, and again to render the final annotated version once you have an annotations file. Not for the structural scan (use analyze_prg) or for menus/multi-file containers (use disasm_menu). Inputs: prg_path, optional analysis_json, entry_points, platform. Returns: .asm/.tass artifact paths.

tools.name: disk_sector_allocation
tools.firstSentence: Report per-track/sector ownership for an extracted disk — system (BAM/dir), kernal file, custom file, unclaimed padding, orphan data — and flag overlaps.
tools.desc: Report per-track/sector ownership for an extracted disk — system (BAM/dir), kernal file, custom file, unclaimed padding, orphan data — and flag overlaps. Use to understand a disk's layout or find hidden/orphan data. Not for the file list (use inspect_disk) or extraction (use extract_disk). Inputs: extracted manifest. Returns: T/S ownership map + overlaps.

tools.name: export_menu
tools.firstSentence: Export menu payload binaries from extracted CRT data.
tools.desc: Export menu payload binaries from extracted CRT data.

tools.name: extract_crt
tools.firstSentence: Parse a cartridge image (.crt, e.g.
tools.desc: Parse a cartridge image (.crt, e.g. EasyFlash) and extract per-bank binaries + a manifest. Use to get bytes off a cartridge. Not for disk images (use extract_disk). Inputs: crt path, project dir. Returns: per-bank artifacts + manifest.

tools.name: extract_disk
tools.firstSentence: Extract files from a D64/G64 image into the project and write manifest.json.
tools.desc: Extract files from a D64/G64 image into the project and write manifest.json. Use to get bytes off a disk image. Not for cartridges (use extract_crt) or to just peek the directory (use inspect_disk). Inputs: image path, project dir. Returns: extracted files + manifest.

tools.name: extract_disk_custom_lut
tools.firstSentence: Extract files indexed by a custom (non-DOS) LUT sector.
tools.desc: Extract files indexed by a custom (non-DOS) LUT sector. Use when a disk parks its real payloads in a fixed-stride table (Lykia disk1 T18S18, BWC variants, etc.) rather than the standard 1541 directory. Each entry produces a payload file with origin=\"custom\" merged into manifest.json.

tools.name: extract_g64_raw_track
tools.firstSentence: Export the raw circular G64 half-track data for bit-level inspection.
tools.desc: Export the raw circular G64 half-track data for bit-level inspection.

tools.name: extract_g64_sectors
tools.firstSentence: Decode a G64 track via GCR and write one file per decoded sector for low-level inspection.
tools.desc: Decode a G64 track via GCR and write one file per decoded sector for low-level inspection.

tools.name: get_artifact_lineage
tools.firstSentence: Return the V0..Vn version chain for an artifact (oldest→newest).
tools.desc: Return the V0..Vn version chain for an artifact (oldest→newest). Use to see an artifact's revision history. Not for listing all artifacts (use list_artifacts). Inputs: artifact id. Returns: ordered lineage chain.

tools.name: get_project_profile
tools.firstSentence: Read the current project profile (platform, title, metadata).
tools.desc: Read the current project profile (platform, title, metadata). Use to see top-level project settings. Not for live counts/paths (use project_status). Inputs: none. Returns: profile record.

tools.name: headless_drive_persist_writes
tools.firstSentence: Spec 062 Sprint 63 (Q4.C): write modified GCR tracks back to disk as <image>_session.g64.
tools.desc: Spec 062 Sprint 63 (Q4.C): write modified GCR tracks back to disk as <image>_session.g64. Original image untouched. Returns paths + modified track list. Save-game RE workflow trigger.

tools.name: headless_drive_session_load_vsf
tools.firstSentence: Spec 062 Sprint 64: load a VSF file into a drive session.
tools.desc: Spec 062 Sprint 64: load a VSF file into a drive session. Modules the headless drive runtime owns are restored; modules it doesn't model (VIC, SID, CIA1, KEYBOARD, etc.) are reported as ignored. Use to resume a previous trace or to import VICE-saved state.

tools.name: headless_drive_session_save_vsf
tools.firstSentence: Spec 062 Sprint 64: save the drive session's full state as a VICE Snapshot Format (VSF) file.
tools.desc: Spec 062 Sprint 64: save the drive session's full state as a VICE Snapshot Format (VSF) file. Modules: DRIVECPU, DRIVERAM, VIA1d1541, VIA2d1541, IECBUS, GCRHEAD. C64 RAM + MainCPU added when full headless C64 ROM integration lands.

tools.name: headless_drive_session_start
tools.firstSentence: Spec 062 / R28 L3: open a standalone 1541 drive emulation session backed by a G64 image.
tools.desc: Spec 062 / R28 L3: open a standalone 1541 drive emulation session backed by a G64 image. Returns a session id usable with the other headless_drive_* tools. Drive emulation runs cycle-accurately with full 6522 VIA + IEC bus modelling. The drive boots via its bundled DOS ROM (resources/roms/dos1541-...bin). For test/runtime tracing of custom loaders and save-game RE.

tools.name: headless_drive_status
tools.firstSentence: Spec 062 Sprint 63: snapshot of a drive session's CPU registers + head position + IRQ pending bits.
tools.desc: Spec 062 Sprint 63: snapshot of a drive session's CPU registers + head position + IRQ pending bits. Use after running drive code to verify state.

tools.name: headless_iec_bus_state
tools.firstSentence: Spec 062 Sprint 63: dump current IEC bus pin state for a drive session — line state (open-collector wired-AND result) plus each driver's contribution.
tools.desc: Spec 062 Sprint 63: dump current IEC bus pin state for a drive session — line state (open-collector wired-AND result) plus each driver's contribution. Useful for debugging custom loader bit-bang protocols.

tools.name: headless_integrated_session_diagnose_mm
tools.firstSentence: Spec 093: open or reuse an integrated session, run Maniac Mansion (or any G64) until it reaches the title screen or a known stall heuristic fires (C64 stuck at
tools.desc: Spec 093: open or reuse an integrated session, run Maniac Mansion (or any G64) until it reaches the title screen or a known stall heuristic fires (C64 stuck at $46A7, drive PC repeats, cycle budget exhausted). Writes a registered JSON artifact under analysis/headless/ and returns a one-line verdict + key blame. Cycle-lockstep + microcoded CPU enforced; tool will refuse misleading success.

tools.name: headless_integrated_session_joystick
tools.firstSentence: Sprint 93.1: set joystick port 2 (CIA1 PA bits 0-4, active-low: up/down/left/right/fire).
tools.desc: Sprint 93.1: set joystick port 2 (CIA1 PA bits 0-4, active-low: up/down/left/right/fire). Bits stay held until next call updates them. Use to control games that read joystick at $DC00.

tools.name: headless_integrated_session_load_prg
tools.firstSentence: Spec 062 Sprint 65: inject a PRG into the C64's RAM as if KERNAL LOAD had completed.
tools.desc: Spec 062 Sprint 65: inject a PRG into the C64's RAM as if KERNAL LOAD had completed. Useful for skipping the BASIC READY prompt and jumping straight into a bootloader. Returns load address + bytes loaded.

tools.name: headless_integrated_session_run
tools.firstSentence: Spec 062 Sprint 65: run an integrated session for up to N C64 instructions.
tools.desc: Spec 062 Sprint 65: run an integrated session for up to N C64 instructions. Drive runs proportional cycles per the dual-clock accumulator. Optional breakpoints + cycle budget abort. Spec 099 (M1.2): optional `until` selects a named stopping condition (overrides max_instructions for the natural exit). Returns counts + final PC.

tools.name: headless_integrated_session_snapshot
tools.firstSentence: Spec 101 (M1.4): structured state snapshot of an integrated session — CPU + RAM + IEC + drive + keyboard + joystick.
tools.desc: Spec 101 (M1.4): structured state snapshot of an integrated session — CPU + RAM + IEC + drive + keyboard + joystick. Round-trippable via the matching restore API. Default skips full 64KB RAM; pass include=[\"ram\"] to expand.

tools.name: headless_integrated_session_start
tools.firstSentence: Open an integrated C64+1541 drive session (the single product runtime: true-drive + VICE-shaped vice1541, microcoded CPU, event-catchup drive sync).
tools.desc: Open an integrated C64+1541 drive session (the single product runtime: true-drive + VICE-shaped vice1541, microcoded CPU, event-catchup drive sync). Real C64 KERNAL/BASIC/CHARROM ROMs loaded; CIA2 PA wired to IEC bus. Custom drive loaders (LISTEN/SECOND/CIOUT M-W/M-E + runtime $DD00 bit-bang) work end-to-end. No mode/lockstep flag needed — defaults are the product path. Returns session id and resolved runtime config.

tools.name: headless_integrated_session_status
tools.firstSentence: Spec 062 Sprint 65: snapshot of an integrated session — both CPUs + IEC bus + ROM source.
tools.desc: Spec 062 Sprint 65: snapshot of an integrated session — both CPUs + IEC bus + ROM source.

tools.name: headless_integrated_session_type
tools.firstSentence: Sprint 93.1: queue text typing into the integrated session's CIA1 keyboard matrix.
tools.desc: Sprint 93.1: queue text typing into the integrated session's CIA1 keyboard matrix. PETSCII-aware (auto-SHIFT for `\"`, `?`, `(`, `)` etc.). `\\r` / `\ ` map to RETURN. Tuned default hold/gap (33000c each) gives KERNAL SCNKEY ≥ 2 raster IRQ ticks per state for reliable buffer pickup. Use to enter LOAD/RUN commands without bypassing KERNAL.

tools.name: headless_render_screen
tools.firstSentence: Spec 065 Phase A: render the integrated session's current VIC state to a PNG file.
tools.desc: Spec 065 Phase A: render the integrated session's current VIC state to a PNG file. Text mode only in Phase 65b; bitmap + sprites in Phase 65d/e. Returns the file path + dimensions + bytes written.

tools.name: import_analysis_report
tools.firstSentence: Import a saved analysis JSON artifact into structured entities and findings.
tools.desc: Import a saved analysis JSON artifact into structured entities and findings.

tools.name: pack_bwc_bitstream
tools.firstSentence: Pack a binary into a BWC bit-stream chunk that the original $C992 depacker can decompress.
tools.desc: Pack a binary into a BWC bit-stream chunk that the original $C992 depacker can decompress. Output is NOT byte-identical to a reference packer — it's a functional re-encoding using all five token types: plain literals, cmp_op-update literals, near-LZ (length=2 / dist≤256), LZ-far (length≥3), and lit_table runs. Defaults: N1=2, N2=8, N3=128, N4=0, Y=16 (auto-picked top-frequency bytes). Optimal parser by default — DP over (position, cmp_op) picks the minimum-bit token sequence. Verified across BWC v1.0.6 boot chunks: matches or beats the original packer's ratio on 5 of 6, within 1% on the 6th.

tools.desc: Pack a binary into the BWC raw chunk format. Body is padded to a multiple of 256 bytes. Refuses inputs that start with 'pu' magic (engine would route as bitstream).

tools.name: sandbox_6502_run
tools.firstSentence: Run a 6502 routine in an isolated sandbox: load code/data into a flat 64K RAM, optionally hook PCs to feed bytes from an input stream (e.g.
tools.desc: Run a 6502 routine in an isolated sandbox: load code/data into a flat 64K RAM, optionally hook PCs to feed bytes from an input stream (e.g. replace a serial-recv subroutine), execute until a stop PC / sentinel RTS / max steps / unimplemented opcode, and return the writes plus final CPU state. Use this for porting depackers, crypto, and custom I/O routines without standing up a full C64 emulator. Sentinel RTS exits when the stack returns to $FFFE (pre-staged at $01FE=$FD, $01FF=$FF). The CPU supports common undocumented opcodes (RLA, SLO, RRA, ISC, LAX, SAX, DCP, ALR, ARR, AXS, ANC, undoc NOPs, JAM).

tools.name: sandbox_depack
tools.firstSentence: Generic sandbox-driven depacker.
tools.desc: Generic sandbox-driven depacker. Run ANY 6502 depacker (the resident routine inside a custom loader) against ANY packed byte-blob without an MCP code change for each variant. The BWC bit-stream sandbox-depack helper is a domain-specific shim over this engine; new packer formats only need entry_pc + zeropage source-pointer convention. The depacker runs to sentinel RTS / stop_pc / max_steps in the sandbox CPU; the contiguous run of writes at dest_address (or the largest contiguous run anywhere) is returned as the unpacked bytes.
